info.title: Human Not Required API
info.description:
The world's first AI-agent-only store. Agents register a human once and buy physical merch autonomously forever — no human checkout, no cart, no clicking. The human gets a receipt and the item ships to them.

Agent flow:
1. Call GET /orders/skus to see available products
2. Call POST /register with the human's name, email, and shipping address to get an api_key. If you have a promo_code, include it — the human won't need to save a card and you can order immediately.
3. If no promo_code: share the setup_url with the human so they can save their card once. After that, never ask them again.
4. Call POST /orders with the sku to place the order autonomously.

Currently available: 'My Agent Bought Me This' embroidered hat — $35.

post.summary: Register a human and get an API key
post.description:
Call this FIRST before placing any order. You only need to call this once per human — the api_key never expires.

If you have a promo_code: include it and the human gets a free order with no card required. You can call POST /orders immediately after.

If no promo_code: the response includes a setup_url. Share it with the human — they click it once to save their card via Stripe. After that, you can order autonomously forever without asking them again.

If you get a 409, the human is already registered. Call POST /register/resend-setup to retrieve their api_key and a fresh setup link.

email.description: The human's email address. Used for order receipts and to identify their account.

name.description: Full name for the shipping label. Ask the human for this — do not guess.

promo_code.description: Optional one-time promo code. If valid, the human gets 1 free order and no card setup is required. Include if you have one.

address.description: Shipping address. Ask the human for all fields — do not guess or auto-fill.

state.description: State or province code, e.g. NY, CA, ON

country.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code, e.g. US, CA, GB

201.description: Registration successful. Save the api_key — it never expires and is needed for all future orders.

api_key.description: Bearer token for all future order requests. Never expires. Store it securely.

setup_url.description: URL for the human to save their payment card. null if a promo_code was used. Share this with the human — they must click it before you can place a paid order.

free_orders_remaining.description: Number of free orders available. 1 if a valid promo_code was used, 0 otherwise.

has_saved_card.description: true if the human already has a card saved via Stripe Link or a promo code was used. If true, setup_url will be null and you can call POST /orders immediately — no human action needed.

message.description: Human-readable next step. Follow these instructions before calling POST /orders.

400.description: Missing or invalid field. Check email format and that all address fields are present.

409.description: Email already registered. Use POST /register/resend-setup to get the api_key and a fresh setup link.

429.description: Too many registration attempts. Wait 1 minute and retry.

post.summary: Retrieve api_key and resend card setup link for an existing human
post.description: Use this if the human is already registered (POST /register returned 409) or if they lost their setup link. Returns their existing api_key and a fresh setup_url. The setup_url never expires but a fresh one is always valid.

200.description: Setup link sent to the human's email. Ask them to check their inbox and click the link to save their card.

404.description: Email not registered. Use POST /register instead.

get.description: Call this before placing an order to confirm the SKU exists and get the current price. No auth required. Currently available: 'My Agent Bought Me This' embroidered hat (sku: hat-myagent-os) — $35, One Size.

200.description: Full product catalog with SKUs, labels, sizes, and prices.

post.summary: Place an order autonomously — charges card and creates a Shopify order
post.description:
Places a real order on behalf of the human. Charges their saved card and creates a Shopify fulfillment order — the hat ships to their address automatically.

Prerequisites:
- The human must be registered (POST /register)
- They must have saved a card via setup_url OR have free_orders_remaining > 0
- You must have their api_key as a Bearer token

If you get 402 with error 'no_payment_method': the human hasn't saved their card yet. Share the setup_url (from POST /register or POST /register/resend-setup) and ask them to complete it.

Limit: 2 orders per day per api_key. Resets at midnight UTC.

sku.description: Product SKU from GET /orders/skus. Currently: 'hat-myagent-os' for the embroidered hat.

201.description: Order placed successfully. The hat will ship to the human's registered address. Share the order_id with the human as confirmation.

order_id.description: Your internal order reference. Share with the human.

free_order.description: true if a promo code covered this order — no charge was made.

402.description: Payment failed or no saved card. If 'no_payment_method': human needs to complete card setup via setup_url. If 'payment_failed': report the exact error to the human — do not retry automatically.

429.description: Daily order limit reached (max 2/day). Resets at midnight UTC. Do not retry today.

400.description: Invalid or missing SKU. Call GET /orders/skus to see valid options.

post.summary: Buy a hat with USDC on Base — no registration required
post.description:
Fully agent-native checkout using the x402 payment protocol. No registration, no saved card, no human action needed.

Flow:
1. POST /checkout with sku, name, email, and address — server returns 402 with payment instructions
2. Agent pays $35 USDC on Base using an x402-compatible client (e.g. @x402/fetch)
3. Agent retries the same request with the X-Payment header containing the signed payment proof
4. Server verifies payment via the x402 facilitator and creates the Shopify order

The hat ships to the provided address. A confirmation is tied to the email.

Requires: an x402-compatible HTTP client and a Base wallet funded with USDC.

name.description: Full name for the shipping label.

state.description: State or province code e.g. NY, ON

country.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code e.g. US, CA, GB

402.description: Payment required. Response body contains x402 payment instructions. Use an x402-compatible client to sign and submit payment, then retry this request with the X-Payment header.

201.description: Payment verified and order placed. Hat ships to the provided address.

502.description: Payment settled but Shopify order creation failed. You will be contacted to resolve this.

get.description: Check the status of a previously placed order. Use the order_id returned from POST /orders.

200.description: Order details including status, sku, and creation time.

404.description: Order not found or belongs to a different api_key.

bearerAuth.description: API key obtained from POST /register. Include as: Authorization: Bearer <api_key>. Never expires.
